html:
A Mandrake is a very rare, passive Mob found in Grasslands and Caves. They are very useful, and on average only 2-5 Mandrakes will spawn per World. They often spawn together in the same area, so finding one will usually result in finding them all. Mandrakes can be used to make the Pan Flute. They can also be used to make Mandrake Soup in a Crock Pot.

When picked at Dusk or Night (or when underground), it will turn into a living Mandrake, following the player around and constantly making noise until morning, after which it will replant itself on the spot. When picked during the Day, it will die instantly and can be obtained as an item. Mandrakes cannot be grown and therefore there is a limited number of them in a world.

With normal damage modifiers, it takes one hit from a Spear to kill a Mandrake (any version of an Axe or Pickaxe will do the same).

Behavior

When picked during dusk or night, rooted Mandrakes will follow the player (through Wormholes as well), hopping around and making a lot of noise. Like Pigs, they will back away when the player gets too close. If the Mandrake is attacked, they will run away from the player indefinitely. Once it becomes daytime, the mandrake will root itself back into the ground, where the player can then pick the mandrake and add it to their inventory.

Food

Raw or Cooked Mandrakes do not spoil. Raw Mandrakes heal 60 Health and 75 Hunger when eaten, and put all nearby Mobs to sleep (until the next cycle of the day; from dusk until night, from night until daytime, from daytime until dusk).

Cooked Mandrake

Cooking a Mandrake will also put the player to sleep (until the next cycle of the day), as well as putting all nearby Mobs to sleep. Eating a Cooked Mandrake restores 100 Health and 150 Hunger, as well as putting the player and all nearby Mobs to sleep (for exactly one day). Eating Mandrake Soup restores 100 Health, 150 Hunger, and 5 Sanity, but does not put the player to sleep.

Pigs, Gobblers, and Pengulls will eat Cooked Mandrakes left on the ground, putting themselves and all nearby Mobs to sleep until the start of the next day (no matter what time it is). Raw Mandrakes will not put the player to sleep. Pigs will also produce Manure (as they would with other Vegetable) if a Mandrake is eaten. Gobblers and Pengulls will eat Mandrakes that are still planted in the ground, putting all nearby Mobs to sleep (except the player).

Downloadable Content

In the Shipwrecked DLC, Mandrakes can be found in the Meadow Biome. They are renewable by using a Trawl Net to obtain a Pan Flute, then using a Deconstruction Staff to dismantle it. Must have merged worlds using the Seaworthy or Skyworthy.

In the Hamlet DLC, Mandrakes can be found in the Deep Rainforest Biome near Mandrake Hills. Mandrakes also can be bought in 'The Sty' Oddities Emporium for 50 Oincs, which makes them renewable. See also Elder Mandrake.

Tips

  • Since Mandrakes are extremely fast creatures and will keep their distance from the player even if they follow them, it's recommended to only pick them during the day if this is the item the player wants. If one is picked at dusk while the player has no weapons, it is best to let it replant itself in the morning because punching the Mandrake will cause it to run away from the player, where it might be killed and eaten by something else.
  • When picking a planted Mandrake during Dusk or Night, the player can try to force attack it by clicking on the Mandrake with the mouse while holding the force attack key.
  • Live Mandrakes can be eaten by Pengulls, Gobblers, and Werepigs, which will still trigger the sleep effects. Gobblers can eat an unpicked Mandrake from the ground.
  • A riled-up Abigail will not attack a live mandrake.
  • Changing settings so that mandrakes are set to lots allows the player to obtain many mandrakes. Because cooking and eating them each set the time forward one day, they can be used to unlock all the characters quickly.
  • One should usually eat cooked mandrakes as they are instead of making mandrake soup since it does not spoil and the sleep effect can be beneficial.
    • Despite this, the player is not advised to do so, as Mandrakes are hard to renew (in Rog there renewable because they can spawn in the caves which can be reset (need investigation for DS))

Trivia

  • The Mandrake (raw and cooked) is one of the few foods in the game that do not Spoil, along with raw/hatching Tallbird Eggs, Guardian's Horn, and Deerclops Eyeball. However, Mandrake Soup will still spoil.
  • Ghosts will often float above Mandrakes.
  • On post "Relese Notes 66529" there was description "There are also reports of a mysterious new creature hiding somewhere on the island. We found the following sound recording in the remnants of Wilson’s camp:" after that there was a Gramaphon with text "Wilson's field recording 1069". When user clicks on Gramaphon, it starts making Mandrake sounds. [1]
  • There is a page on the Klei website that is nothing more than multiple looping gifs of hopping Mandrakes along with looping audio of the "meep" sound they make.
  • The Mandrake, Mandragora officinarum as per Wickerbottom's identification, is a real-life plant that has roots in European folklore. Its roots can bear a vaguely human shape and were said to scream when uprooted. The sleep effect caused by cooking and eating Mandrakes is based on the myth that those who hear the scream of a mandrake die, which itself is based around the fact that the real plant can cause unconsciousness when eaten in sufficient quantities.

Bugs

  • Even though Mandrakes are flammable, they will not turn into Ashes when killed with a Fire Dart.
  • (PC) When the player picks up a Mandrake using the Spacebar, the Mandrake can be planted again, but if the player tries to pick the Mandrake when it has been replanted, the character will start to shake out of control upon attempting and will be unable to interact with the Mandrake again.
  • (PC) Mandrakes spawned into the player's inventory can be replanted when 'dropped' on the ground. Stacks of Mandrakes will be replanted as if there is only one, and when picked at dusk or night, only one Mandrake will actually follow the player. If killed, a stack of however many Mandrakes was originally spawned will be the number the player receives.
  • If a Mandrake runs into a Tooth Trap, the game may crash.
  • In SW, sometimes a Mandrake can follow the player into the Ocean, essentially walking on water. At daybreak, it will plant itself in the water.
  • Mandrakes in the player's inventory can sometimes be lost when using the Seaworthy to cross over to SW.

Gallery

  • Original HD Mandrake icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Original HD Cooked Mandrake icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Dead, planted, and recently deceased Mandrakes.
  • Two hopping Mandrakes.
  • A Mandrake found underground.
  • Three mandrakes following Wilson.
  • A Mandrake mid-hop.
  • Hounds asleep due to eating Mandrakes.
  • A drawing of Wendy and Webber taking pictures of a live Mandrake from Rhymes With Play.
  • A Mandrake plush.
  • The Mandrake is a mystery accessory for the Blind Box Figures.

“I've heard strange things about those plants.”
–Wilson, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“That's not a normal plant.”
–Willow, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“Is strange plant.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“The mighty mandrake. I should pick it at night.”
–Wendy, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“IT IS A PLANT”
–WX-78, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“Mandragora officinarum.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“It's looking at me.”
–Woodie, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“What a disturbing root.”
–Maxwell, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“The appearance of that root raises my curiosity.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“A rutabaga!”
–Wigfrid, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“That's a funny looking plant.”
–Webber, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“A mystical plant.”
–Walani, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“Have I discovered a new root vegetable?!”
–Warly, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“A plant o'legend.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“'TIS CARROT?”
–Wilba, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“Hiding”
–Wormwood, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“Swell. I picked up an annoying sidekick.”
–Wheeler, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“That's a weird shrub.”
–Winona, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“It's a prank plant.”
–Wortox, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“Supposed to pick those at night, florp.”
–Wurt, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“Hey, what's that hiding down there?”
–Walter, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“What is that, a turnip?”
–Wanda, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“Cut it out!”
–Wilson, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Now that's just creepy!”
–Willow, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Little plant man friend!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Onward, my vegetable minion!”
–Wendy, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“WHAT DO YOU WANT?”
–WX-78, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Mandragora officinarum. But with a face.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“What have I done?”
–Woodie, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Oh shut UP, will you?”
–Maxwell, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Its screech is of a remarkably annoying register.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“She just wants tö gö ön a rutabaga saga.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“You're a bad friend!”
–Webber, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“You're messing up my chill vibes, dude.”
–Walani, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Do not pick! Do not pick!”
–Warly, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Quit yer squawkin'!”
–Woodlegs, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“IT DOTH PROTEST TOO MUCH, METHINKS”
–Wilba, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Merp! Merp! Meep! Merp!”
–Wormwood, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Give me one good reason why I shouldn't just launch you out of the Pew-matic.”
–Wheeler, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“This is exactly what having a little sister's like.”
–Winona, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Stop that or I'll steal your voice.”
–Wortox, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Glargh! It following me!”
–Wurt, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Follow the leader!”
–Walter, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“You can stop chiming in any time!”
–Wanda, when examining a picked Mandrake.

In the Shipwrecked DLC, Mandrakes can be found in the Meadow Biome. They are renewable by using a Trawl Net to obtain a Pan Flute, then using a Deconstruction Staff to dismantle it. Must have merged worlds using the Seaworthy or Skyworthy.

Since Mandrakes are extremely fast creatures and will keep their distance from the player even if they follow them, it's recommended to only pick them during the day if this is the item the player wants. Despite this, the player is not advised to do so, as Mandrakes are hard to renew (in Rog there renewable because they can spawn in the caves which can be reset (need investigation for DS))

Ghosts will often float above Mandrakes.

We found the following sound recording in the remnants of Wilson’s camp:" after that there was a Gramaphon with text "Wilson's field recording 1069". When user clicks on Gramaphon, it starts making Mandrake sounds. [1]
There is a page on the Klei website that is nothing more than multiple looping gifs of hopping Mandrakes along with looping audio of the "meep" sound they make.

The sleep effect caused by cooking and eating Mandrakes is based on the myth that those who hear the scream of a mandrake die, which itself is based around the fact that the real plant can cause unconsciousness when eaten in sufficient quantities.

Stacks of Mandrakes will be replanted as if there is only one, and when picked at dusk or night, only one Mandrake will actually follow the player. Mandrakes in the player's inventory can sometimes be lost when using the Seaworthy to cross over to SW.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Nhân Sâm là một Mob thụ động rất hiếm được tìm thấy ở Đồng cỏ và Hang động. Chúng rất hữu ích và trung bình chỉ có 2-5 Nhân Sâm xuất hiện trên mỗi Thế giới. Chúng thường sinh sản cùng nhau trong cùng một khu vực, vì vậy việc tìm thấy một con thường sẽ dẫn đến việc tìm thấy tất cả. Nhân sâm có thể được sử dụng để làm Sáo thần Pan. Chúng cũng có thể được sử dụng để nấu Súp Nhân Sâm trong Nồi Hầm.
title: Mandrake

Merp! |wheeler = Give me one good reason why I shouldn't just launch you out of the Pew-matic. |winona = This is exactly what having a little sister's like. |wortox = Stop that or I'll steal your voice. |wurt = Glargh! It following me! |walter = Follow the leader! |wanda = You can stop chiming in any time! |choose = maxwell |source = when examining a picked Mandrake.}} A '''Mandrake''' is a very rare, passive [[Mob]] found in [[Grassland]]s and [[Caves]]. They are very useful, and on average only 2-5 Mandrakes will spawn per [[World]]. They often spawn together in the same area, so finding one will usually result in finding them all. Mandrakes can be used to make the [[Pan Flute]]. They can also be used to make [[Mandrake Soup]] in a [[Crock Pot]]. When picked at [[Day-Night Cycle|Dusk]] or [[Night]] (or when [[underground]]), it will turn into a living Mandrake, following the player around and constantly making noise until morning, after which it will replant itself on the spot. When picked during the [[Day]], it will die instantly and can be obtained as an item. Mandrakes cannot be grown and therefore there is a limited number of them in a world. With normal damage modifiers, it takes one hit from a [[Spear]] to kill a Mandrake (any version of an [[Axe]] or [[Pickaxe]] will do the same). == {{Pic|32|Brain}} Behavior == When picked during dusk or night, rooted Mandrakes will follow the player (through [[Wormhole]]s as well), hopping around and making a lot of noise. Like [[Pigs]], they will back away when the player gets too close. If the Mandrake is attacked, they will run away from the player indefinitely. Once it becomes daytime, the mandrake will root itself back into the ground, where the player can then pick the mandrake and add it to their inventory. {{clear}} == Food == {{Object Infobox |foodValue = {{Pic|32|Mandrake}} × 1 |canBeBurned = 1 |foodType = [[Veggie]] [[File:Veggie.png|32px|]] |image = Mandrake Dropped.png |icon = Mandrake.png |name = Mandrake |hpRestored = 60 |hungerRestored = 75 |sanityRestored = 0 |spoil = Does not spoil |stack = 40 |droppedBy = {{pic|40|Mandrake Mob|Mandrake}} |soldBy ={{Ham}}: {{pic|32|'The Sty' Oddities Emporium}} ({{Pic|24|Oinc}} ×50) |spawnCode = "mandrake" }} {{Quotes |wilson = Mandrake roots have strange properties. |willow = Why did this plant have a face? |wolfgang = Little plant man is died! |wendy = Alas, poor mandrake! |wx78 = I HAVE KILLED IT. GOOD. |wickerbottom = Mandragora officinarum. Deceased. |woodie = Sorry, little guy. |maxwell = My ears are still ringing. |wagstaff = It appears to be adverse to light rays. |wigfrid = The cörpse öf the rutabaga still retains its magic. |webber = The skin is all seared. |walani = I was rooting for it. |warly = I should like to get to the root of this mystery... |woodlegs = Twas a noisy little beast. |wilba = 'TIS DEAD |wormwood = Need friend? |wheeler = Forgive me if I don't mourn your death. |winona = Dead as a doornail. |wortox = It's out of prank juice. |wurt = It not moving anymore. |walter = It's got a little face! |wanda = Oh, I can't bear to look at it... |choose = wigfrid |source = when examining a dead Mandrake.}} Raw or Cooked Mandrakes do not spoil. Raw Mandrakes heal 60 [[Health]] and 75 [[Hunger]] when eaten, and put all nearby [[Mobs]] to sleep (until the next cycle of the day; from dusk until night, from night until daytime, from daytime until dusk). {{clear}} ===Cooked Mandrake=== {{Object Infobox |canBeBurned = 1 |foodType = [[Veggie]] [[File:Veggie.png|32px|]] |image = Cooked Mandrake Dropped.png |icon = Cooked Mandrake.png |name = Cooked Mandrake |hpRestored = 100 |hungerRestored = 150 |sanityRestored = 0 |spoil = Does not spoil |stack = 40 |spawnCode = "cookedmandrake }} {{Quotes |walter=Sorry little buddy. |wanda=It was a matter of survival. |warly=Could use horseradish... |wigfrid=She's definitely dead. |maxwell=It has lost a lot of its power. |webber=Cooked to death. |wendy=It was a good death. You will be delicious. |wickerbottom=Mandragora officinarum, cooked in the name of discovery. |willow=The fire didn't get rid of its face. Gives me the willies. |wilson=Poor little guy. |winona=Dead as several doornails. |wolfgang=Little plant man is food? |woodie=Was that a bad thing to do? |wormwood=Smells sleepy |wortox=It's at peace now. |wurt=Feel bit weird eating veggie with face, florp. |wx78=THAT'S WHAT YOU GET FOR BEING PATHETIC |walani=Not as mystical as I thought. |wagstaff=Smells faintly of thebaine. |woodlegs=Woodlegs cooked it up! |wheeler=Just looking at it makes me yawn. |wilba=FWOOSHINGS DOTH QUIET IT |choose=wx78 }} [[Cooking]] a Mandrake will also put the player to sleep (until the next cycle of the day), as well as putting all nearby Mobs to sleep. Eating a Cooked Mandrake restores 100 Health and 150 Hunger, as well as putting the player and all nearby Mobs to sleep (for exactly one day). Eating [[Mandrake Soup]] restores 100 Health, 150 Hunger, and 5 Sanity, but does not put the player to sleep. [[Pig]]s, [[Gobbler]]s, and [[Pengull]]s will eat Cooked Mandrakes left on the ground, putting themselves and all nearby Mobs to sleep until the start of the next day (no matter what time it is). Raw Mandrakes will not put the player to sleep. Pigs will also produce [[Manure]] (as they would with other [[Vegetable]]) if a Mandrake is eaten. Gobblers and Pengulls will eat Mandrakes that are still planted in the ground, putting all nearby Mobs to sleep (except the player). {{clear}} == {{pic|32|Icon Tools}} Usage == {{Recipe |item = Mandrake |tool = Cooking |result = Cooked Mandrake |note = Will put the player to sleep. }} {{Recipe |item1 = Mandrake |item2 = Filler |item3 = Filler |item4 = Filler |tool = Crock Pot |result = Mandrake Soup }} {{Recipe |item3 = Cut Reeds |count3 = 5 |item2 = Rope |item1 = Mandrake |tool = Prestihatitator |result = Pan Flute }} {{Recipe |item = Cooked Mandrake |manure = yes |result = Manure |note = Will put all nearby Mobs to sleep. Cannot be given directly. }} =={{Pic|32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content== In the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC, Mandrakes can be found in the [[Meadow]] [[Biome]]. They are renewable by using a [[Trawl Net]] to obtain a [[Pan Flute]], then using a [[Deconstruction Staff]] to dismantle it. Must have merged worlds using the [[Seaworthy]] or [[Skyworthy]]. In the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC, Mandrakes can be found in the [[Deep Rainforest]] Biome near [[Mandrake Hill]]s. Mandrakes also can be bought in [['The Sty' Oddities Emporium]] for 50 [[Oinc]]s, which makes them renewable. See also [[Elder Mandrake]]. =={{Pic|32|Prototype}} Tips== * Since Mandrakes are extremely fast creatures and will keep their distance from the player even if they follow them, it's recommended to only pick them during the day if this is the item the player wants. If one is picked at dusk while the player has no weapons, it is best to let it replant itself in the morning because punching the Mandrake will cause it to run away from the player, where it might be killed and eaten by something else. * When picking a planted Mandrake during Dusk or Night, the player can try to force attack it by clicking on the Mandrake with the mouse while holding the force attack key. * Live Mandrakes can be eaten by [[Pengull]]s, [[Gobbler|Gobblers]], and [[Werepig]]s, which will still trigger the sleep effects. Gobblers can eat an unpicked Mandrake from the ground. * A riled-up [[Abigail]] will not attack a live mandrake. * Changing settings so that mandrakes are set to lots allows the player to obtain many mandrakes. Because cooking and eating them each set the time forward one day, they can be used to unlock all the characters quickly. * One should usually eat cooked mandrakes as they are instead of making mandrake soup since it does not spoil and the sleep effect can be beneficial. ** Despite this, the player is not advised to do so, as Mandrakes are hard to renew (in Rog there renewable because they can spawn in the caves which can be reset (need investigation for DS)) == {{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * The Mandrake (raw and cooked) is one of the few foods in the game that do not [[Spoil]], along with raw/hatching [[Tallbird Egg]]s, [[Guardian's Horn]], and [[Deerclops Eyeball]]. However, Mandrake Soup will still spoil. * [[Ghost]]s will often float above Mandrakes. * On post "Relese Notes 66529" there was description "There are also reports of a mysterious new creature hiding somewhere on the island. We found the following sound recording in the remnants of Wilson’s camp:" after that there was a Gramaphon with text "Wilson's field recording 1069". When user clicks on Gramaphon, it starts making Mandrake sounds. (Archive) [https://web.archive.org/web/20121114065654/http://www.dontstarvegame.com/blog/ Don't Starve blog] in September 2012 * There is a [https://web.archive.org/web/20150315152837/http://www.dontstarvegame.com/science/mandrake/meep.html page] on the Klei website that is nothing more than multiple looping gifs of hopping Mandrakes along with looping audio of the "meep" sound they make. * The Mandrake, ''[[Wikipedia:Mandragora officinarum|Mandragora officinarum]]'' as per Wickerbottom's identification, is a real-life plant that has roots in European folklore. Its roots can bear a vaguely human shape and were said to scream when uprooted. The sleep effect caused by cooking and eating Mandrakes is based on the myth that those who hear the scream of a mandrake die, which itself is based around the fact that the real plant can cause unconsciousness when eaten in sufficient quantities. == {{pic|32|Mosquito}} Bugs == * Even though Mandrakes are flammable, they will not turn into [[Ashes]] when killed with a [[Blow Dart#Fire Dart|Fire Dart]]. * (PC) When the player picks up a Mandrake using the Spacebar, the Mandrake can be planted again, but if the player tries to pick the Mandrake when it has been replanted, the character will start to shake out of control upon attempting and will be unable to interact with the Mandrake again. * (PC) Mandrakes spawned into the player's inventory can be replanted when 'dropped' on the ground. Stacks of Mandrakes will be replanted as if there is only one, and when picked at dusk or night, only one Mandrake will actually follow the player. If killed, a stack of however many Mandrakes was originally spawned will be the number the player receives. * If a Mandrake runs into a [[Tooth Trap]], the game may crash. * In SW, sometimes a Mandrake can follow the player into the [[Ocean]], essentially walking on water. At daybreak, it will plant itself in the water. * Mandrakes in the player's inventory can sometimes be lost when using the Seaworthy to cross over to SW. =={{Pic|32|Blueprint}} Gallery== CD mandrake.png|Original HD Mandrake icon from Bonus Materials from [[CD Don't Starve]].
